_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L1 baseline cavalry lancer (mounted, kettle-helm, mustache, yellow+red split surcoat over brown leather long-sleeves, wooden spear with iron leaf-tip, brown leather reins, chestnut horse with red+blue checkered caparison) and produce the L2 'Outfitted Lancer' variant (Tier 1 mid).
_meta.input_image: UnitProgression/Cavalry/Refs/L1_Base.png (1024x1024 native, downscaled from a 2048x2048 source — backup at Refs/L1_Base_2K.png; LOCKED 2026-05-06)
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Outfitted Lancer beat = 4 visible adds (iron studs along the leather waist belt, iron pommel cap on the wooden spear haft butt, iron-buckled bridle on the horse with iron browband, hip pouch on rider's anatomical-RIGHT hip). Mirrors Infantry L1->L2 prompt skeleton with Cavalry-specific anchors (kettle-helm, handlebar mustache no beard, yellow+red split surcoat over brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket, vertical wooden spear with iron leaf-tip, brown leather reins gripped at horse withers, chestnut horse with white blaze and red+blue checkered caparison). Single-input pass from Refs/L1_Base.png since L2 is the chain origin (no prior composite exists yet).

v2 (2026-05-07): strip all 'white blaze' references on the horse — solid chestnut brown across the entire body with NO white markings (no blaze, no star, no socks, no speckling). Locked 2026-05-06 after L5 keeper-pick (the model amplified any white area on the muzzle into broader markings). Authoring rule: never describe white anywhere on the horse. No other content changes; AD FeedbackV0 left L2 adds untouched.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v4 (2026-05-07): trimmed L2 from 4 adds to 3 — dropped IRON STUDS ALONG THE LEATHER WAIST BELT positive add per AD detail-economy ("embossed studs ... use clean wider band instead" first-cut item). The studs would have persisted through every later level as permanent surface ornament; removing them from L1->L2 leaves the belt as plain warm-brown leather with the existing brass buckle only.
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.summary: add small dark-iron square buckles to the leather bridle on the horse's head: one buckle on the cheekstrap (the strap running from behind the horse's ear down to the bit), one buckle on the throatlash (the strap running under the horse's jaw), for a total of two visible...

adds.title: HIP POUCH ON THE RIDER'S ANATOMICAL-RIGHT HIP
adds.summary: add a small rectangular brown leather pouch with a flap closure hanging from the new studded leather belt on the rider's anatomical-RIGHT hip (the viewer-LEFT side of the belt line, on the same side of the body as the spear arm).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/Refs/L1_Base.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible additive upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the input image, matching the warm-brown leather + dark-iron palette of his existing kit. The three upgrades together represent an 'Outfitted Lancer' rank step — the conscript light lancer has finished outfitting his basic levy kit. (1) IRON POMMEL CAP ON THE WOODEN SPEAR HAFT BUTT — add a small dark-iron cylindrical cap at the BUTT end of the wooden spear haft (the bottom end of the haft, opposite the iron leaf-tip at the top). The cap is matte dark-iron, roughly 1.5 inches tall, wrapping the full circumference of the haft butt with a flat or slightly domed end. Visible at the lower end of the spear haft below the gripping hand. The wooden haft itself remains warm-brown and unchanged everywhere else. (2) IRON-BUCKLED BRIDLE ON THE HORSE WITH IRON BROWBAND — add small dark-iron square buckles to the leather bridle on the horse's head: one buckle on the cheekstrap (the strap running from behind the horse's ear down to the bit), one buckle on the throatlash (the strap running under the horse's jaw), for a total of two visible square iron buckles on the viewer-RIGHT side of the horse's head. ALSO add a slim dark-iron browband across the horse's forehead between the eyes and the ears, replacing the existing leather-only browband — the new browband is a thin matte dark-iron strap roughly half an inch wide, riveted at each end to the cheekstraps. The rest of the leather bridle (cheekstraps, noseband, headstall) remains warm-brown leather, unchanged. (3) HIP POUCH ON THE RIDER'S ANATOMICAL-RIGHT HIP — add a small rectangular brown leather pouch with a flap closure hanging from the existing brown leather waist belt on the rider's anatomical-RIGHT hip (the viewer-LEFT side of the belt line, on the same side of the body as the spear arm). The pouch is about three inches tall and two inches wide, matte warm-brown leather with a clearly visible flap top secured by a small brown leather strap and a tiny brass stud. The pouch hangs at the rider's hip level, roughly between the saddle and the lower edge of the surcoat hem. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: his chiseled chibi jawline; his confident flat closed-mouth expression with no smile and no snarl; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with a soft rosy blush on the cheeks; his THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth approximately half an inch past each corner — the mustache stays handlebar-shaped, NOT bushy, NOT walrus, NOT extending into a beard; his cheeks and chin are CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow visible; his dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle-helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the steel kettle-helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with a forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes by approximately one and a half inches, open face with the entire face fully visible below the brim. The kettle-helm is smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail, NOT chain, NOT ring mesh. THE RIDER TORSO: the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split SURCOAT — viewer-LEFT half of the chest is mustard-yellow, viewer-RIGHT half of the chest is crimson-red, vertical seam down the center of the chest, simple round neckline, hem reaches the natural waist where the brown leather belt sits — this yellow+red split surcoat livery remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, NOT covered, NOT shrunk, NOT replaced. The brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket beneath the surcoat — both arms covered shoulder to wrist in matte warm-brown leather with soft painted shading, no studs, no armor, no metal, no quilted pattern. The wide brown leather waist belt with a square brass buckle centered at the front — the brass buckle stays at the existing position; the belt remains plain warm-brown leather with no studs, no rivets, no embossed dots. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the brown leather long sleeves stay covering both arms shoulder to wrist; both hands bare warm peachy-tan skin (the anatomical-RIGHT hand grips the wooden spear haft just above shoulder height; the anatomical-LEFT hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers); no bracers, no vambraces, no gauntlets at this level. THE RIDER LEGS: the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff with soft painted vertical fold lines (visible under the saddle skirt on the viewer-LEFT side); the chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf with a darker folded ankle cuff at the top and wide flat soles, both boots in stirrups. THE WEAPON: the long wooden spear with iron leaf-tip — heavy warm-brown wooden haft running approximately six feet long, neutral gray (matching the existing kettle-helm tone, balanced gray) iron leaf-shaped spearhead at the top of the haft (broad leaf shape approximately eight inches long, mounted on the wooden haft via a visible iron socket), held vertically in the rider's anatomical-RIGHT hand at shoulder height with the spear-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. The wooden haft stays warm-brown unchanged everywhere except the new iron pommel cap at the butt end. THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit (concealed in the horse's mouth) back to the rider's anatomical-LEFT hand at the horse's withers — both reins matte warm-brown leather, gripped in a relaxed grip, unchanged. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame and rump and tail on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the viewer-RIGHT side of the head with a single bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — the muzzle, forehead, neck, chest, belly, legs, hooves, and rump are ALL the same warm chestnut brown color. NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze on the muzzle, no white star on the forehead, no white socks on the legs, no white speckling. Dark brown mane along the top of the neck falling toward the viewer side at the withers approximately three inches long, dark brown tail extending behind the rump and falling to about hock height. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, and tail all remain identical in shape, color, and position to the input image. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with a small pommel at the front and a low cantle at the back, visible just above the caparison line, unchanged. The brown leather bridle on the horse's head — cheekstraps, noseband, headstall all matte warm-brown leather — stays warm-brown, but the existing leather-only browband is replaced with the new dark-iron browband per Edit Goal item (2), and the new dark-iron square buckles are added to the cheekstrap and throatlash per Edit Goal item (2). The bit (concealed in the horse's mouth) stays unchanged. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison draped over the horse's back covering rump, flanks, and chest with a red and blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares approximately three inches per square) and a steel-blue trim band along the lower edge, the lower edge cut into shallow rectangular tabs, draping to about knee-height on the horse — the caparison's pattern, colors, layout, and tabbed lower edge are UNCHANGED, identical to the input image. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees toward the viewer's left so the rider's anatomical right side faces camera. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT — head and muzzle on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame, rump and tail on the viewer-LEFT side. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the hand grips the wooden spear haft just above shoulder height; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the iron leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas; the knuckles of the gripping hand are visible to camera. THE REIN ARM (anatomical-LEFT, on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder; the rein hand is visible to camera holding the reins in a relaxed grip. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance: spear shouldered upright but not couched, reins held but not pulled. The pose is NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop — both the rider and horse are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses (under the horse's belly, under the rider's chin, behind the rider's leg on the viewer-LEFT), slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the input image exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide, with the horse rump on the viewer-LEFT and the horse muzzle on the viewer-RIGHT defining the horizontal envelope). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas (~117px headroom — the wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the iron leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge, so the spear-tip remains visible in the upper portion of the canvas). BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas (~112px below). Rider+horse vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. Approximately 21.6% empty margin viewer-LEFT, approximately 17.8% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves, DO NOT shrink the spear so its tip falls below the helm, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow under the horse's hooves, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split surcoat — the yellow+red chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, identical in panel layout to the input image; DO NOT replace the surcoat with a body coat, gambeson, robe, mantle, or armor; DO NOT cover the surcoat chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor at this level; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, cuirass, or any body metal armor — the only metal at is the existing kettle-helm, the existing iron leaf-tip on the spear, the new iron pommel cap at the spear butt, the new iron browband and bridle buckles on the horse, and the existing brass belt buckle; DO NOT add a closed helm, sallet, half-visor, full visor, helm crest, helm horns, or any structural addition to the kettle-helm — the helm stays exactly as the existing kettle-helm; DO NOT remove the kettle-helm or replace it with a different helm; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT render any new metal piece in cool blue-gray steel, bluish steel, icy silver, chrome, OR overly warm/brown-tinted steel — every new metal addition must match the NEUTRAL gray tone of the existing kettle-helm and spear leaf-tip (balanced gray, NEITHER blue-shadowed NOR brown-shadowed; soft painted highlights that read clean-white, neither cool nor warm); DO NOT change any facial feature including the jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, mustache shape, mustache length, or skin tone; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or add a five o'clock shadow — the cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven with NO beard at all; DO NOT change the hair color or hair fringe under the helm; DO NOT change the surcoat colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color (dark blue / muted steel-blue) or boot color (warm brown); DO NOT add metal sabatons, greaves, leg armor, or any leg metal at this level (those arrive at this level); DO NOT swap the hand gripping the spear; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the reins; DO NOT change the spear shape, the wooden haft length, the iron leaf-tip shape, the iron leaf-tip size, or replace the wooden haft with a steel lance haft (steel haft arrives at this level); DO NOT add iron banding to the spear haft or wrap the haft with leather grip cord (those arrive at this level / respectively); DO NOT change the orientation of the spear from vertical with tip pointing up; DO NOT add a pennon flag or banner to the spear (that arrives at this level); DO NOT change the brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket to bare biceps, scale-mail sleeves, or studded leather; DO NOT add bracers, vambraces, or gauntlets at this level; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, or sword belt at this level; DO NOT add steel pauldrons, shoulder caps, or any shoulder armor at this level (a single pauldron arrives at this level); DO NOT add gold trim, gold studs, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere — the brass belt buckle remains brass (its existing color), but no new gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add a knife sheath at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add a second hip pouch or a pouch on the anatomical-LEFT hip — exactly ONE pouch at the anatomical-RIGHT hip at this level; DO NOT change the brown leather waist belt to a different color or material — it stays warm-brown leather with the existing brass buckle ONLY (no iron studs, no rivets, no embossed dots, no decorative metalwork on the belt at); DO NOT change the brown leather saddle, brown leather reins, or saddle pommel/cantle (those gain iron banding at higher tiers, not at this level); DO NOT add a peytral, chamfron, crinet, flanchard, barding, or any horse armor at this level (peytral arrives at this level, chamfron at this level, crinet at this level, flanchards at this level); DO NOT change the horse coat color from chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white forehead patch, no white muzzle patch, no white socks, no white speckling, no white spots, no white shading anywhere on the horse; DO NOT change the horse's pose (calm four-square standing, head facing viewer-RIGHT, neck arched, ears forward); DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move in any way; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species — the horse remains a real chibi chestnut-brown horse with proper anatomy (head, ears, eye, mane, neck, body, four legs, hooves, tail); DO NOT change the horse's mane shape, tail shape, ear shape, or neck arch; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, the steel-blue trim band, the tabbed lower edge, the size of the squares, or the drape shape — the caparison stays identical to the input image; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio of either the rider or the horse; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure or the horse; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane (other than the existing minimal contact shadow), or add cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
